title: Add Site Photos column, Point of Contact, and Project Manager fields

requirements.summary: Add SITE PHOTOS column before SIGNATURE column in PDF table layout with embedded base64 thumbnails
acceptanceCriteria: The generated PDF includes a SITE PHOTOS column header
The SITE PHOTOS column appears to the left of the SIGNATURE column
Uploaded site photos are rendered as embedded images within the SITE PHOTOS column cells in the PDF

file_operations.description: Modify the generatePDF function to add a SITE PHOTOS column in the table layout. Position the new column immediately before the existing SIGNATURE column. Within the SITE PHOTOS column cells, embed the site photo thumbnails as base64 images using the sitePhotos array from ReportData. Ensure proper column width allocation and image scaling to fit within the cell boundaries while maintaining aspect ratio.

requirements.summary: Add Point of Contact and Project Manager input fields with localStorage persistence and PDF inclusion
acceptanceCriteria: ReportForm displays a labeled 'Point of Contact' text input field
ReportForm displays a labeled 'Project Manager' text input field
Both fields persist their values to localStorage
Both fields' values appear in the generated PDF report under project info or a dedicated section
Fields are cleared/reset when the form is reset

file_operations.description: Extend the ReportData interface to include pointOfContact and projectManager as optional string fields. Update the generatePDF function to display these values in the project information section of the PDF, positioned appropriately among the existing project metadata fields.

Add pointOfContact and projectManager state fields to the component. Add labeled text input controls for 'Point of Contact' and 'Project Manager' in the project information section of the form, positioned logically near other project metadata fields. Update the localStorage save and load logic to persist these two new fields alongside existing form data. Include both fields in the ReportData object passed to generatePDF. Ensure both fields are cleared when the form reset handler is invoked.
